name: Paulo Luigi's Pasta Fagioli
recipeInstructions:
This recipe was handed down to me from my mother. It can be made with canned beans to save time. Use 4 cans of beans for each pound dried. Soak the beans overnight in a 5-quart pot (kettle) with twice as much water as beans. The next day, drain the beans and add fresh water just covering them. Boil until cooked through (1-1/2 hours), stirring occasionally using a wooden spoon to ensure even cooking. Keep beans warm.
Generously coat a frying pan with olive oil. Add onion and sauté until translucent. Stir in garlic and basil leaves, sautéing for 1-2 minutes while constantly stirring. Add flour and cook for one minute. Gradually add water (about half a cup), stirring until thickened (1-2 minutes). Stir in tomato paste until dissolved.
Remove from heat and gently stir into the warm beans. Adjust thickness by adding water as desired, then stir until well mixed. Season with pepper and salt to taste. Simmer over low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Cook pasta al dente (4-5 minutes) and drain. Place in a soup bowl, add the hot bean mixture, mix gently, and garnish with parsley. Serves 6-8 people.
